What is the problem?: The app works for login temple search and maps but is NOT safe or complete enough to publish yet.
How to fix it: Fix all rows marked Must fix = Yes first. If you are in a hurry release on Android only not iPhone yet.

What is the problem?: Your project has a mobile app web admin and shared code folders. Structure is fine.
How to fix it: No action needed. Optional: write a short note in a README explaining which folder is which.

What is the problem?: The project is not set up with Git. You cannot easily track changes or see what was uploaded by mistake.
How to fix it: Run git init. Add a .gitignore file. Never upload passwords API keys or keystore files.

What is the problem?: There are many extra copy files ending in _1 (example: package_1.json). They create confusion.
How to fix it: Search for files named *_1.* and delete the copies. Keep only the main file.

What is the problem?: Firebase and API code exists in two different folders. They can get out of sync.
How to fix it: Pick one folder for shared code and delete or stop using the duplicate.

What is the problem?: There is no main guide explaining how to install and run the project.
How to fix it: Create a README at the project root with steps: install Node install packages run Android app.

What is the problem?: The mobile app README is still the default template not written for TirthDarshi.
How to fix it: Update apps/CleanDD/README.md with your real app name and how to build the app.

What is the problem?: There is no sample env file showing which keys the app needs. Keys are written directly in code instead.
How to fix it: Create a .env.example file listing variable names only (no real keys). Move real keys into .env files.

What is the problem?: Nothing runs tests automatically when code changes. Bugs can slip into releases.
How to fix it: Set up GitHub Actions (or similar) to run npm test and npm run lint on every change.

What is the problem?: No system automatically builds the Play Store or App Store package.
How to fix it: Set up a build pipeline that creates a signed Android .aab file (and iOS build if needed) using secrets stored safely.

What is the problem?: Only one test exists and it fails. Important flows are not tested.
How to fix it: Fix Jest config so tests can run. Add basic tests for login and loading temples. Run npm test until it passes.

What is the problem?: iPhone test file still checks for old demo text from the React Native template.
How to fix it: Update or delete the old test in ios/CleanDDTests.

What is the problem?: The web admin panel has almost no tests.
How to fix it: Add tests only if you plan to use the web admin in production.

What is the problem?: Running lint shows warnings and at least one error in App.js.
How to fix it: Open App.js fix the hook warning. Clean up lint warnings over time.

What is the problem?: The app prints many debug messages (console.log) that users should not see in production.
How to fix it: Remove debug logs or wrap them so they only show during development. Test the release build.

What is the problem?: TempleSearchScreen.js is almost 3000 lines. Hard to maintain and fix bugs.
How to fix it: Split it into smaller files: search logic map logic and UI components.

What is the problem?: If the app crashes in production you will not get reports.
How to fix it: Add Firebase Crashlytics or Sentry. Wrap the app in an error screen so it does not show a blank white screen.

What is the problem?: Android release build is mostly set up (version 1.0.9 compression on). Some unused settings remain.
How to fix it: Build a release APK/AAB and test on a real phone. Remove unused lines in build.gradle.

What is the problem?: Your Play Store signing password is written in build.gradle as 123456. Anyone with the code can see it.
How to fix it: Remove passwords from build.gradle. Store them in environment variables or your CI secret vault. Use a strong password.

What is the problem?: The file thirtdarshi.keystore sits inside the app folder. If leaked someone can publish fake updates.
How to fix it: Keep the keystore only on your secure machine or CI. Do not share it publicly. If it was ever uploaded create a new key.

What is the problem?: Android targets the latest required API level (35). Good for Play Store.
How to fix it: No action needed. Check Google Play policy each year for updates.

What is the problem?: Internet and location permissions are set correctly. Background location was removed which helps Play Store approval.
How to fix it: When submitting to Play Store explain that you use location for temple navigation.

What is the problem?: Firebase config file exists and includes your app package name but also old package names.
How to fix it: In Firebase Console confirm package com.thirtdarshi.app is registered with your release certificate fingerprint.

What is the problem?: A release was built before but no final .aab file was found ready to upload.
How to fix it: Run a full release build: cd android then ./gradlew bundleRelease. Test the output file before upload.

What is the problem?: Code shrinking is on but some rules still say com.yourapp instead of your real package name.
How to fix it: In proguard-rules.pro change com.yourapp to com.thirtdarshi.app. Test release build for crashes.

What is the problem?: Your Google Maps API key is saved in gradle.properties in plain text.
How to fix it: Move the key to a local file that is not shared. In Google Cloud restrict the key to your app package and certificate only.

What is the problem?: local.properties contains your personal computer paths. It should not be shared.
How to fix it: Make sure local.properties is never uploaded to Git. Each developer creates their own.

What is the problem?: The iPhone folder and Xcode project exist. Basic structure is there.
How to fix it: No action if releasing Android only. For iPhone you need the fixes in rows 29-31.

What is the problem?: GoogleService-Info.plist is missing. Login and database will not work on iPhone.
How to fix it: In Firebase Console add an iOS app. Download GoogleService-Info.plist and put it in apps/CleanDD/ios/CleanDD/.

What is the problem?: Apple requires a sentence explaining why you need location. Yours is blank so App Store will reject the app.
How to fix it: Open Info.plist and fill NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription with text like: We use your location to show nearby temples and give directions.

What is the problem?: Android shows TirthDarshi but iPhone still shows CleanDD.
How to fix it: Change CFBundleDisplayName in Info.plist to TirthDarshi to match your brand.

What is the problem?: A privacy file exists on iPhone but collected data types are not listed.
How to fix it: Review Apple privacy requirements and fill in what data you collect (email location etc).

What is the problem?: Firebase API keys are copied into JavaScript files. Anyone can extract them from the published app.
How to fix it: Add Firestore security rules (row 36). Turn on Firebase App Check. Create new keys in Firebase if old ones were public.

What is the problem?: The key for driving directions is in the app code. Others could use it and run up your bill.
How to fix it: In Google Cloud restrict the key. Better: move direction requests to a small server you control.

What is the problem?: Anyone who signs up with email admin@temple.com gets admin powers. This is checked only inside the app not on the server.
How to fix it: Use Firebase Custom Claims: only your team gets an admin flag on the server. Block admin actions in Firestore rules.

What is the problem?: There are no security rule files. If rules in Firebase Console are open anyone could read or change temple data.
How to fix it: Create firestore.rules that allow: users read temples; only admins write. Deploy rules with Firebase CLI.

Area: Passwords and keys may be shared
What is the problem?: Some files try to hide secrets but keys are still inside code that could be uploaded.
How to fix it: Remove all real keys from code. Rotate (replace) any key that was ever in Git or shared. Use .env for secrets.

What is the problem?: iPhone is configured to block unsafe HTTP connections. Good for security.
How to fix it: No action needed.

What is the problem?: Users can sign up and log in with email and password. Validation and error messages work.
How to fix it: Optional: require email verification before full access.

What is the problem?: Sign in with Google is set up but must be tested on a real release build.
How to fix it: Build a signed release APK. Test Google login. Add release SHA-1 fingerprint in Firebase Console if it fails.

What is the problem?: New users must tick Terms and Conditions before creating an account.
How to fix it: No action needed. Also add the same terms link on the Play Store listing.

What is the problem?: A Terms and Conditions screen exists with privacy and location sections.
How to fix it: Optional: ask a lawyer to review the text before launch.

What is the problem?: Play Store and App Store require a public privacy policy web page. None was found in the project.
How to fix it: Publish a privacy policy on your website (or GitHub Pages). Paste that URL in Play Console and App Store Connect.

What is the problem?: Maps navigation still shows placeholder text Your Company Name instead of your real company.
How to fix it: In App.js update termsAndConditionsDialogOptions with your real company name and legal text.

What is the problem?: Users must log in before using temple features. Guest access is blocked.
How to fix it: No action needed unless you want guest browsing later.

What is the problem?: The app downloads every temple from the database then filters on the phone. Fine for now slow when data grows.
How to fix it: Later: add database queries so the server returns only what is needed.

What is the problem?: Turn-by-turn navigation is integrated. Needs testing on real roads and slow phones.
How to fix it: Test navigation end-to-end. Watch Google Cloud billing for Maps usage.

What is the problem?: The app supports English and Telugu in many places.
How to fix it: Review all screens for missing translations before launch.

What is the problem?: A no internet screen was built but is not connected to the app. It also needs a missing package.
How to fix it: Either: install netinfo package and wrap App.js with NetworkCheck — or delete NetworkCheck.js if not needed.

What is the problem?: The admin page on mobile will crash: imports are commented out wrong file picker API and wrong file read function.
How to fix it: In AdminScreen.js: uncomment imports from firebaseController; use DocumentPicker.pick(); use RNFS.readFile() for CSV. Test add edit delete.

What is the problem?: The website admin panel looks more complete than the phone admin screen.
How to fix it: Decide: use web admin only for staff and fix phone admin OR remove phone admin from release.

What is the problem?: A small server folder exists but is broken and not used by the main app.
How to fix it: Ignore for launch if the app uses Firebase directly. Or fix imports and add package.json later.

What is the problem?: Some code still mentions localhost:3001 but the app uses Firebase instead.
How to fix it: Remove or update old localhost URLs to avoid confusion.

What is the problem?: React Native and Firebase versions are modern.
How to fix it: Run npm audit occasionally and update packages with security fixes.

What is the problem?: Some installed packages are not used or reference missing packages.
How to fix it: Remove unused packages like idb from mobile app. Fix or delete DivyaDesamsApp if unused.

What is the problem?: No one has run npm audit to check for known vulnerabilities in packages.
How to fix it: In apps/CleanDD run: npm audit. Fix high and critical issues.

What is the problem?: package.json says 0.0.1 but Android says 1.0.9.
How to fix it: Pick one version number and use it everywhere.

What is the problem?: Internal name is CleanDD public name is TirthDarshi.
How to fix it: Decide final name for Play Store icon and listings. Use TirthDarshi everywhere users see it.

What is the problem?: Google Play and Apple require forms describing what data you collect (email location etc).
How to fix it: In Play Console fill Data Safety form honestly. For Apple fill App Privacy section. Match your privacy policy.

What is the problem?: Android is closer to ready than iPhone. Security and admin issues block both.
How to fix it: Recommended order: (1) Fix database rules and keys (2) Fix admin screen (3) Fix signing and secrets (4) Add privacy policy URL (5) Build and test release AAB (6) Submit to Play Store. Do iPhone after rows 29-30 are fixed.
